reviews.review_description: The game seems to be nice and, so far, interesting. The gameplay is original, it has great graphics, and the music/sounds are good too. I just started it and I'm still on the third patient. But I found two things that, in my opinion, disrupt the experience. The first is the resource creation system, which, if you play sitting down, is too far away, and to interact with the tools you have to stretch your back too much, so it's not very comfortable. The second is in the gameplay (but this could be subjective), where you don't have the freedom to advance, and having so many things to do all at once leads to panic and mistakes. These two factors (for me) make the gameplay quite heavy. If you have long arms and thrive on hurry, you'll play it without problems. Otherwise, it will cause a bit of frustration. Still, I give it a good rating for originality and a touch of culture.

I was not sure about this one for a while, as I thought it could be using biology terms as decoration for usual monster fight. but once I tried it, it turned out to be really nice. it's not a biology course, so do not expect many details, but I would say it stays true to the scientific representation of processes in the cell, on this simplified level. and it's fun. and it quickly gets frantic. actually, I got stuck at some point, as I am not as effective as a living cell X)

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and for your awesome review!

reviews.review_title: Can't recall first lesson in the game
reviews.review_description: I never did biology at school. It's a challenge.
Played it on normal mode and it was a good challenge. Looking forward to trying the two harder modes

If somebody had told me  “you will remotely pilot a macrophage to destroy parasites, viruses, and other nasties, learn about various cell organelles and their functions, and sequence nucleotides to create protein blueprints for fighting off more of the nasties”, I would have thought this game holds more educational  value than fun.  But no:  this game is a blast and has a little bit of everything. It’s creepy with the occasional bits of humor, the voiceover guidance is clear and concise, and the convincing narrative results in sympathy for the afflicted. The educational component is bundled seamlessly with the fun factor and can be reinforced by replaying at the higher difficulty levels (there are 4: trainee, normal, hard, and intense).  Lighthaus knocked it out of the park with this one and I’ll be watching closely for others like it.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the fantastic feedback.  We’re so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
Started the game and I have a left hand glitch right on my head the whole time, I can't get rid of it. It's not my
controller or anything on my side. I get off the game and everything else is normal.. any bug fix updates?

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear this. It must be a bug from a Quest operating system or firmware update because we haven’t changed anything in the build — and we got a report yesterday of this happening on our other game too. Both have been out for years and never had this issue. Hopefully Meta fixes it soon. (Also, please make sure your headset has the latest firmware update).  Sorry for the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description:
In my opinion this is making the most of VR. It's actually quite comfortable as you can play it seated, it can be played for a longer time without feeling bad.

The fact that it's educational and using VR in a somewhat challenging but doable way is just perfect in my opinion. Definitely recommend!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the fantastic feedback!

reviews.review_description: When I was a kid I used to watch The Magic School Bus when I came home from school. I never thought that I would see the day when I would be able to virtually shrink down to a nano-sized particle and fight off invading pathogens from the inside of a macrophage. Drawing heavily on biology topics including DNA replication, immune response, and functions of cell organelles, you will be surprised at how educational fighting off pandoraviruses using a sword-shaped defensin protein can be! It’s such a polished experience and something I will definitely be showcasing to my high school science classes!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the fantastic review!  We’re so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: This game is very underrated, well done devs!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the vote of confidence!

reviews.review_description:
I’ve just completed the first three patients in “Nano,” and I’m already hooked. This VR game masterfully blends nerdy science with immersive gaming, creating an experience that’s both educational and exhilarating. As a trainee in a bioresearch program, you control a nanodrone injected into the human body to fight infections at the microscopic level. The use of real science in the game’s DNA makes it incredibly engaging, teaching you about cellular systems while you play.

The game’s ability to balance fun with learning is impressive, making it standout in VR. I’m eager to continue my journey and uncover the mystery behind the global virus. If you’re a fan of science and VR, “Nano” is the game. ✊🏽.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your fantastic review! We’re so glad you enjoyed it! Keep going, you gotta get the virus — if you can!

reviews.review_description:
I just finished the game, and I think it’s a great game! The gameplay is easy to understand, while still being challenging when coming across waves of enemies. There were times when the enemies got a bit overwhelming, but after learning from my mistakes, the fights became a breeze.
The educational aspect is pretty neat, too. However, it is a bit simplified. I don’t see this as a bad thing. It’s a neat way to teach people about the cell and immune system in a very graspable way.
The soundtrack was good, but I think a bit more variety would have been music to my ears (no pun intended). Maybe a different track for different types of germs. The final boss music was pretty killer, as well!
Overall, “Nano” is a fun experience that’s well worth the $15 I paid for it. I’d love to see a sequel or an expansion of the game in the future!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing and for your great review and feedback!

reviews.review_title: Amazing blend of education and fun!
reviews.review_description: As someone who majored in biology and took molecular cell biology this app is amazing! First and foremost it’s a fun game. While you are having fun you learn about the interior of the cell and how different organelles work while defending against a COVID like pandemic. It really is cool, especially if you’re into biology at all.

Unfortunately, there are no German subtitles for this very innovative game with instructive content and well-made graphics, so I have difficulty following the instructions and learning the lessons in a relaxed manner. The developers should deliver subtitles for the common languages as an update as soon as possible. It's very incomprehensible why this wasn't integrated into such a good educational game from the start.
I would like to know if there is another game that is similar to this game as I loved the educational aspect of this. Higher recommended!
Top !👍

It has a bit of an educational angle. But is mostly a 13-level-fun game where you have to handle different objects inside a cell in order to react to outside threats. Can get you to sweat a bit.

Clearly deserves more attention. Because I suspect that a lot of people don't realize what a fun game it is until they get to know it. It may have been a mistake by the devs to give the impression that this was more of an educational software than it actually is. That said, it also teaches some basic things on cells.

reviews.review_title: I feel like I’m missing something
reviews.review_description: Is there only the one version with like 15 patients then that’s it? I finished in like 3 days

This is one of the best educational games I’ve seen in a while that teaches you about the foundations of biology at the cellular level in the disguise of a video game. I need to get my family to play it, and it’s a nice refresher “course” for me.

This game started off quite promising but after a lot of repetition and trying to remember the sequence of performing tasks in a particular order I got stuck. There were no hints or help to move forward so I eventually gave up. It's a shame because potentially this could have been a really good game.

I start to play this thing and after what seemed like an hour of exposition, I start "playing" it. Before anything even happens, it wants me to do A.  But to make A work, I have to do B. Then C. Then D. Then E. And it is clear that I am supposed to REMEMBER to do all this in the right sequence and remember the scientific names.

When it asked me to do F, I exited, un-installed and asked for a refund.

So I’m studying for my CPT and figured I’d give this a go. 

While it is engaging, and challenging I don’t really retain much of what I’m taught. Maybe if I kept at it (took a break for now) it’ll be repetitive enough to stick. But the teaching part isn’t all that thorough. 

I find myself stressed as I’m trying to retain knowledge and beating a mission. I’m slow and don’t react well at the slightest to pressure.

Every mission involves in interacting with a lot of different mechanics in creating/feeding/building and destroying.

It’s engaging and the story makes sense. It is a fun way to maybe learn something if you’re a bit different than myself. 

L I N K T R , 
                    E E
                          /
                            D I S C O U N T
                                                  F U R Y

Came across this game when searching the store and was pleasantly surprised by how much information is accurate to cellular and molecular biology when I was playing! I am a PHD student, and  seeing a game that incorporates many of the topics I've studied in undergrad and grad school was a blast! The information is not too deep, and there is enough to peek a student's interests and curiosity. I hope to find more games like this!
I like this game very much, but please make it also in german language.

I have never before experienced such a compelling mix of science and gaming. You can never forget the inner working of a cell after playing Nano! And you will start appreciating how much hard work is required just to keep you alive. This game should be mandatory in any high school biology course!

This game is well polished and a good value for money. I'm about 80% finished after a few hours and enjoying it.

I have only a couple small issues. I had quite a hard time catching the glucose molecules and I think it's because I play in a seated position. I find if I reach up high, I can catch them pretty easily but if I try catching them closer to me, I mostly can't grab them.
Maybe in the trainee mode at least, a bit of time down time could be added after spawning to allow you to build up defenses or alternately begin with more resources present or some already built. The most difficult thing I find is that you start out in such a resource starved condition that it's hard to adequately prepare for the first contact. 

But these are just minor issues and other people may feel differently.

reviews.review_title: Really fun game, especially for a nerd
reviews.review_description: I never thought I would be so excited to see ATP synthase in a vr game, but here we are! Loved the progression in the game, and becoming familiar with the optimal ways to beat each enemy type. My only negative was I wished the game was longer. Felt like I got into the swing of things then suddenly the game was over, but a reasonable length game considering it’s only $15. Will definitely be on the lookout for DLC or a sequel, and guess it’s time to try the harder difficulties!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We’d love to make it longer!

reviews.review_description:
Amaaazing game that uses actual, technical biology, you can impress yor friends later with your understanding of intracellular functioning. Wish we had more games like this.
Can't wait for the sequel!!!

Way above my expectations (and I am doing a PhD in bioinformatics :) ). I expected more of an "interactive tour" into biology with primitive interaction,  but it is an exciting combination of biology and game (emphasis on the second one). It has a good story and game loop. The gameplay becomes a bit repetitive, but its price and level of realization it is not a problem at all.

There is a lot of growth potential, but I am sure devs perfectly know it.

I will be waiting for the future DLC/sequel!
I thought it'd be boring - single user + a bunch of science I know nothing about. But the instructions were very clear and gameplay extremely immersive and fun. Love this game!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review. You kind of captured our main goal: it’s possible to make fun high quality games where people learn cool stuff! Much appreciated!

reviews.review_description: I love the way the game teaches you about the cells and their organelles while playing a strategy game. It is so fun you don't even realize that you are learning about DNA, RNA, mitochondria, etc. I am having an absolute blast playing.

developer_response.body: That's fantastic! We're so glad you're having fun (and learning a coupla things :)....

reviews.review_description:
I don’t know what else to add, it’s just an excellent product! It’s shows a really great use of VR technology to deliver seriously interesting adventure experience with strong educational part.
Just hats off to authors! I hope to see more of such great products from you!🎓👍
please!!

This game is great and I was never very good at biology. I wish they had this game when I was in class I would have learned everything so much easier. You are a nanobot inside of a person's body that is sick helping them function properly until you can find and kill the virus that is attacking them.
 Except this game isn't super easy You really need to be prepared to remember what they tell you. Once I got going I did not want to stop playing this game!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Our team is delighted you liked it!

reviews.review_description: By training I am in physics and optics but I often work on project collaborating with biologist.  I yearned to learn more about our body but had no easy way in.  The terminology I picked up in my job helps me to recognize them in the game.  Now I get to know how that works!  And I realized the game website has more learning tool to get familiarize  which is what.  I would be great and essential for player to dig deeper so the impact would go beyond just "graphical icon recognition" into solid biology learning tool.  I thank you for such a wonderful game to push public science forward.  Better educated citizens make a better world.
Super fun game I love controlling a cell and it’s fun

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! So glad you had fun!

reviews.review_description: Great game, great price, a must try!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! A scrimmage or battle mode is a cool idea!

reviews.review_description: It is amazing to see cell, virus, in action.

This game is absolutely fantastic. There is lots to do and much to learn. The game involves combat, time management, and creative thinking. The graphics are bright, colorful and very well done. I enjoy the narration & storyline as well. All in all it is a phenomenal game. I recommend it to everyone.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, we're delighted you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: I love science and I love this game because I can play and learn at the same time

developer_response.body: Love it, thank you! Our goal was to make a legit fun VR game where you learn some science.
